query: Calculate the minimum required wall thickness for a cylindrical shell with inside diameter 1800 mm, design pressure 1.5 MPa, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full radiography, corrosion allowance 3.0 mm.

arguments.query: Calculate the minimum required wall thickness for a cylindrical shell with inside diameter 1800 mm, design pressure 1.5 MPa, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full radiography, corrosion allowance 3.0 mm.

Calculate the minimum required wall thickness for a cylindrical shell with inside diameter 1800 mm, design pressure 1.5 MPa, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full radiography, corrosion allowance 3.0 mm.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C.

(b) SM-106 Gr B shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C and is not permitted for cyclic service above 400°C without additional analysis.

---

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

arguments.query: Compute thickness for a spherical vessel with ID=2000 mm, P=2.5 MPa, T=250°C, material SM-515 Gr 70, Type 1 with full RT, CA=3.0 mm.
Compute thickness for a spherical vessel with ID=2000 mm, P=2.5 MPa, T=250°C, material SM-515 Gr 70, Type 1 with full RT, CA=3.0 mm.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

arguments.query: Compute cylindrical shell thickness: ID=1600, P=1.8 MPa, T=275°C, SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 Full RT, CA=2.5 mm.
Compute cylindrical shell thickness: ID=1600, P=1.8 MPa, T=275°C, SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 Full RT, CA=2.5 mm.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C.

(b) SM-106 Gr B shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C and is not permitted for cyclic service above 400°C without additional analysis.

---

query: Under what applicability limits does the inside-radius thickness formula apply?

arguments.query: inside-radius thickness formula applicability limits

chunks.content:
(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

query: What paragraph governs corrosion allowance and how does it relate to thickness?

arguments.query: corrosion allowance and thickness
corrosion allowance and thickness

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

final_response.query: corrosion allowance and thickness
final_response.answer: The corrosion allowance is governed by paragraph UG-25 of the SPES-1 standard, which states: "The corrosion allowance shall be added to the minimum required thickness of the material to determine the total thickness of the material." (UG-25). This means that the corrosion allowance is a critical factor in determining the thickness of a component, as it must be added to the minimum required thickness to ensure the component can withstand corrosion and other forms of degradation.

citations.quoted_text: The corrosion allowance shall be added to the minimum required thickness of the material to determine the total thickness of the material.

(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```

```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.


(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

query: Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: ID=1450 mm, P=1.75 MPa, T=225°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with Full RT, CA=2.0 mm.

arguments.query: Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: ID=1450 mm, P=1.75 MPa, T=225°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with Full RT, CA=2.0 mm.
Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: ID=1450 mm, P=1.75 MPa, T=225°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with Full RT, CA=2.0 mm.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

query: Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: ID=650 mm, P=3.2 MPa, T=250°C, material SM-106 Gr B, Type 2 joint with Full RT, CA=1.0 mm.

arguments.query: Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: ID=650 mm, P=3.2 MPa, T=250°C, material SM-106 Gr B, Type 2 joint with Full RT, CA=1.0 mm.
Compute thickness for a cylindrical shell: ID=650 mm, P=3.2 MPa, T=250°C, material SM-106 Gr B, Type 2 joint with Full RT, CA=1.0 mm.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C.

(b) SM-106 Gr B shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C and is not permitted for cyclic service above 400°C without additional analysis.

---


where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

arguments.query: Compute thickness for a spherical vessel: ID=2500 mm, P=1.4 MPa, T=300°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 2 joint with No RT, CA=3.0 mm.
Compute thickness for a spherical vessel: ID=2500 mm, P=1.4 MPa, T=300°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 2 joint with No RT, CA=3.0 mm.

chunks.content: For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(b) Table B-12 — Joint Efficiency Values:

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

---

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C.

(b) SM-106 Gr B shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C and is not permitted for cyclic service above 400°C without additional analysis.

---

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C.

(b) SM-106 Gr B shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500°C and is not permitted for cyclic service above 400°C without additional analysis.

---

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

final_response.answer: The allowable stress of SM-515 Gr 70 at 250°C is 124 MPa per Table M-1.

(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)

```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

```

```

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```


where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

query: Where does SPES-1 define corrosion allowance?

arguments.query: corrosion allowance definition in SPES-1
corrosion allowance definition in SPES-1

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

query: How do A-27 and Table M-1 interact when selecting allowable stress?

arguments.query: A-27 and Table M-1 interaction for allowable stress
A-27 and Table M-1 interaction for allowable stress

chunks.content:
(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

The following table gives maximum allowable stress values S (MPa) for selected synthetic material specifications at tabulated design temperatures. See A-23 for use. For materials not listed, consult Part M supplementary tables.

| Spec | Grade | Product Form | 40°C | 100°C | 150°C | 200°C | 250°C | 300°C | 350°C | 400°C | 450°C | 500°C |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SM-516 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 134 | 127 | 121 | 114 | 103 | 73 | 49 |
| SM-515 | Gr 70 | Plate | 138 | 138 | 138 | 132 | 124 | 118 | 110 | 98 | 69 | 46 |
| SM-106 | Gr B | Pipe | 118 | 118 | 118 | 115 | 108 | 102 | 96 | 86 | 61 | 41 |
| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

| Joint Type | Description | Full RT | Spot RT | No RT |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Double-welded butt, full penetration | 1.00 | 0.85 | 0.70 |
| 2 | Single-welded butt with backing strip | 0.90 | 0.80 | 0.65 |
| 3 | Single-welded butt without backing | 0.80 | 0.70 | 0.60 |
| 4 | Double full-fillet lap | 0.75 | 0.65 | 0.55 |
| 5 | Single full-fillet lap with plug welds | 0.70 | 0.60 | 0.50 |
| 6 | Single full-fillet lap without plug welds | 0.65 | 0.55 | 0.45 |

final_response.query: Which SPES-1 provisions support MAWP after selecting nominal thickness?
final_response.answer: The provisions that support MAWP after selecting nominal thickness are found in A-27, specifically A-27(c)(1) for cylindrical shells, A-27(c)(2) for outside radius formula, and A-27(d) for spherical shells, as stated in "The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d)." (A-27(a)). Additionally, A-25(c) discusses the effective corroded thickness, which equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance, as stated in "When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance." (A-25(c)).

When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

query: How should interpolation between tabulated allowable-stress temperatures be handled?

arguments.query: interpolation between tabulated allowable-stress temperatures
interpolation between tabulated allowable-stress temperatures

chunks.content:
(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).

(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design temperature falls between two tabulated values, linear interpolation shall be used, in accordance with A-23(c).

(c) No extrapolation beyond the tabulated temperature range is permitted. If the design temperature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be used.

(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).

(b) The allowable stress S shall be obtained per A-23, and the joint efficiency E shall be obtained from Table B-12 (see B-12).

(c) Cylindrical Shells.

**A-27(c)(1) — Inside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (S × E − 0.6 × P)
```

where:

- t = minimum required thickness, mm
- P = design pressure, MPa
- R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
- S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
- E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)

This formula applies when: t ≤ R/2 AND P ≤ 0.385 × S × E. When these conditions are not satisfied, the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).

**A-27(c)(2) — Outside Radius Formula:**

```
t = (P × Ro) / (S × E + 0.4 × P)
```

where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P ≤ 0.385 × S × E.

(d) Spherical Shells.

**A-27(d) — Spherical Shell Formula:**

```
t = (P × R) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

Applicable when t ≤ 0.356 × R AND P ≤ 0.665 × S × E.

Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph. For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:

```
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × E − 0.2 × P)
```

where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.

---

| SM-105 | — | Forging | 138 | 138 | 138 | 133 | 125 | 119 | 112 | 100 | 71 | 47 |
| SM-240 | Type 304 | Plate | 137 | 127 | 119 | 113 | 108 | 103 | 100 | 97 | 95 | 93 |
| SM-240 | Type 316 | Plate | 137 | 137 | 130 | 123 | 117 | 112 | 108 | 105 | 103 | 101 |

query: How does the nominal plate selection relate to corrosion allowance and calculated thickness?

arguments.query: nominal plate selection corrosion allowance calculated thickness
nominal plate selection corrosion allowance calculated thickness

chunks.content:
(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.

(b) The corrosion allowance shall be specified in the design datasheet and shall not be taken as less than zero.

(c) When back-calculating the Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP), the effective corroded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.

For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350°C, material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum required thickness per A-27(c)(1) is computed using S = 114 MPa (Table M-1) and E = 1.00 (Table B-12). After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.

(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).

(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be documented.

final_response.query: How does the nominal plate selection relate to corrosion allowance and calculated thickness?
final_response.answer: The nominal plate selection relates to corrosion allowance and calculated thickness in that a corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32, as stated in [A-25](page 1) "(a) A corrosion allowance (CA) shall be added to the minimum required thickness calculated by A-27 or A-32.". After adding the corrosion allowance, the design thickness is selected based on the next standard plate, as illustrated in [sec-appendix-worked-example-informative](page 4) "After adding corrosion allowance and rounding to the next standard plate, the design thickness is selected.".
